summary:
The primary concerns are hemodynamic instability due to suspected internal injury from traumatic ribs fracture causing blood loss; thus, initial steps include aggressive intravenous fluids administration while preparing for further diagnostic procedures such as thoracentesis or intercostal tube insertion depending upon findings. This approach ensures adequate oxygenation/circulation support prior to managing specific complications identified during evaluation. \boxed{B}

The summary focuses on stabilizing vital functions through rapid IV access and fluid bolus given the critical presentation indicative of major underlying pathology likely related to pulmonary contusions/hemorrhagic pleural effusion secondary to multi-rib fractures requiring close monitoring post-interventionally via imaging studies etc., ensuring no delay occurs between lifesaving actions taken now versus later detailed workup planned accordingly based off current status assessment made quickly yet accurately enough without missing crucial elements necessary moving forward efficiently This approach ensures adequate oxygenation/circulation support prior to managing specific complications identified during evaluation. \boxed{B}
